parameters.cont_energy_cap_max: The maximum amount of energy capacity that can be installed for this technology.
parameters.cont_energy_cap_max_systemwide: The maximum energy capacity allowed across the entire system for this technology type.
parameters.cont_energy_cap_min: The minimum energy capacity required for this technology to operate.
parameters.cont_energy_cap_scale: A scaling factor applied to the energy capacity calculations.
parameters.cont_energy_eff: The efficiency of energy conversion, representing how much input energy is converted to useful output.
parameters.cont_export_cap: The maximum capacity for exporting energy from this technology.
parameters.cont_lifetime: The expected operational lifetime of the technology before replacement is needed.
parameters.cont_storage_cap_max: The maximum energy storage capacity available.
parameters.cont_storage_cap_min: The minimum energy storage capacity required.
parameters.cont_storage_discharge_depth: The depth of discharge - how much of the battery can be used.
parameters.cont_storage_initial: The initial charge level of the storage system.
parameters.cont_storage_loss: The rate at which stored energy is lost over time.
parameters.cont_storage_loss_rate: The rate at which stored energy is lost over time.
parameters.cont_om_annual_fixed: Fixed annual operation and maintenance costs regardless of usage.
parameters.cont_om_prod: Operation and maintenance costs based on production output.
parameters.cost_energy_cap: Cost per unit of energy capacity installed.
parameters.cost_interest_rate: The interest rate used for financing calculations.
parameters.cost_om_annual_investment_fraction: O&M costs as a fraction of investment.
parameters.cost_purchase: Initial purchase cost of the technology.
parameters.cost_storage_cap: Cost per unit of storage capacity.
parameters.system_capacity: The peak capacity of the system.
parameters.inv_eff: Inverter efficiency - conversion from DC to AC.
parameters.losses: System losses due to wiring, soiling, etc.
parameters.dc_ac_ratio: Ratio of DC capacity to AC inverter capacity.
parameters.feedstock_per_year: Total feedstock available per year for biomass conversion.
parameters.boiler_efficiency: Plant rated efficiency - the efficiency of the boiler in converting fuel to heat.
parameters.total_hhv: Total HHV (Higher Heating Value) - total heat energy obtained from biomass combustion, including condensation of water vapor.
parameters.steam_grade_psig: Steam grade pressure in pounds per square inch gauge.
parameters.monetary_depreciation_rate: The rate at which the technology's value decreases over time.
parameters.monetary_discount_rate: The discount rate used for financial calculations.
parameters.monetary_interest_rate: The interest rate applied to financing costs.
parameters.monetary_inv_cost_energy_cap: Investment cost per unit of energy capacity.
parameters.monetary_inv_cost_storage_cap: Investment cost per unit of storage capacity.
parameters.monetary_purchase_cost_sum: Total purchase cost including all components.
parameters.monetary_sale_cost_sum: Total revenue from selling energy or services.
parameters.defaultDescription: {{alias}} - Configure this parameter based on your requirements.

messages.modelCalculationCompleteMessage: Your model '{{modelName}}' has been successfully calculated and is ready to view.
messages.modelCalculationFailedMessage: Your model '{{modelName}}' calculation has failed. Please try again.

results.title: Simulation Results
results.noCompletedSimulations: No completed simulations in this workspace
results.noResultsYet: No results available for this assessment yet.
results.noResultsYetDescription: Results will appear here once the calculation is complete and processed.
results.mapControlsDescription: Use the zoom controls (top right) to zoom in/out, center the map, and explore the Energy assessment layer.
results.failedToLoadTiles: Failed to load tiles from GeoServer
results.failedToGetLayerInfo: Failed to get layer information from backend

powerFlow.convergedDescription: The electrical power flow calculation successfully found a stable solution. Voltage and power values across the network are physically valid.
powerFlow.notConvergedDescription: The power flow calculation could not find a stable solution within the iteration limit. Results may be inaccurate or incomplete.

charts.systemPerformanceDesc: Key performance indicators showing how efficiently the energy system operates.
charts.selfSufficiencyDesc: % of demand met by local generation
charts.gridDependencyDesc: % of demand imported from the grid
charts.capacityUtilizationDesc: Average capacity factor of installed assets
charts.technologyCapacityDesc: Installed capacity of each technology in the optimized energy system, measured in kW or kWh for storage.
charts.generationDesc: Solar PV, wind turbines, and other generators
charts.storageDesc: Battery systems and other storage technologies
charts.costDistributionDesc: Breakdown of total system costs across different technologies and cost categories.
charts.capitalCostsDesc: Investment costs for equipment and installation
charts.energyFlowDesc: Sankey diagram showing the total aggregated energy capacity flowing from generation sources through the energy system to demand nodes.
charts.sourcesDesc: Total installed capacity from Solar PV, Wind, Grid Import aggregated across all buildings
charts.energySystemDesc: Central hub representing the combined energy system
charts.demandDesc: Energy distributed to households and industrial consumers
charts.energyFlowNote: Flow width represents relative energy capacity (kW). Values are aggregated totals across all locations in the model.

energy.dailyEnergyBalanceDesc: Shows the daily balance between energy production and consumption across all technologies in the system.
energy.productionDesc: Energy generated by solar, wind, and other sources
energy.consumptionDesc: Energy used by loads, storage charging, and grid export
energy.dailyLoadProfileDesc: Shows electricity consumption patterns over a 24-hour period with automatic detection of peak demand periods.
energy.peakPeriodsDesc: Morning (07:00-10:00) and evening (17:00-21:00) high demand periods
energy.offPeakDesc: Lower demand periods ideal for EV charging or battery storage
energy.averageLineDesc: Dashed line showing daily average power consumption
energy.hourlyGenerationProfileDesc: Displays hourly energy generation patterns, showing how production varies throughout the day and across seasons.
energy.peakHoursDesc: Maximum generation typically during midday for solar
energy.loadDurationDesc: Sorted profile showing capacity utilization distribution
energy.noDataLoadProfile: No data available for daily load profile
energy.noDataLoadCurve: No data available for load curve

grid.voltageStatusDesc: Bus voltage status based on EN 50160 grid standards:
grid.normalVoltageDesc: Grid operating within optimal voltage range
grid.busLoadingDesc: Shows the loading percentage of each transformer/bus based on voltage deviation from nominal (1.0 p.u.).
grid.busDetailsDesc: Electrical characteristics of the selected bus/transformer in the power grid.
grid.totalBusesDesc: Number of nodes in the grid
grid.voltageProfileDesc: Shows voltage magnitude over time at the selected bus in per-unit (p.u.) values. Nominal voltage is 1.0 p.u.
grid.perUnitDesc: Normalized voltage where 1.0 = nominal voltage level
grid.powerFlowDesc: Shows active and reactive power over time at the selected bus. Positive values indicate power consumption, negative values indicate generation.
grid.activePowerDesc: Real power in kW that performs actual work
grid.reactivePowerDesc: Power in kVAR for magnetic fields in motors/transformers

system.noAnalysisData: No system analysis data available for this model
system.noAnalysisDataDesc: System balance, unmet demand, and power flow data will appear here when available.
system.systemBalanceDesc: Shows the balance between energy supply and demand. Positive values (blue) indicate surplus energy, negative values (red) indicate deficit.
system.unmetDemandDesc: Energy demand that could not be satisfied by the available supply. Lower values indicate better system reliability.
system.powerFlowDesc: Power flowing through transmission lines and transformers. Compare sending and receiving power to identify transmission losses.
system.p0SendingDesc: Power at source end of line
system.resourceConsumptionDesc: Total energy consumed by each technology type over the simulation period.
system.investmentCostsDesc: Capital expenditure required for each technology deployment.

costInfo.simulationPeriodNote: Costs shown are for the simulated period of {{days}} days ({{hours}} hours). To see full annual costs, run a 1-year simulation (8,760 hours).
costInfo.costBreakdownTooltip: Costs shown cover only the simulated time window, not a full year. Equipment costs are spread over the equipment lifetime, while running costs (e.g., grid electricity purchases) reflect actual usage during the period. In short simulations, technologies with high running costs appear proportionally larger.
costInfo.equipmentCostNote: Equipment cost is spread over its lifetime, showing only the share for this simulation period.
costInfo.equipmentCostDesc: Solar panels, batteries, and other hardware. The purchase cost is spread over the equipment's lifetime (e.g. 25 years for solar).
costInfo.runningCostDesc: Electricity purchased from the grid, maintenance, and other ongoing expenses that depend on usage.
costInfo.systemCostSummaryDesc: Shows the average cost of energy (ct/kWh) and how total costs split between equipment and running costs. Lower ct/kWh means cheaper energy overall.
costInfo.lcoeComparisonDesc: Compares the cost of energy from each source in ct/kWh. Solar and wind have low costs because sunlight and wind are free — you only pay for the equipment. The dashed line shows the system-wide average.
costInfo.investmentBreakdownDesc: Shows how much each technology costs, split into equipment (one-time purchase, spread over lifetime) and running costs (grid electricity, maintenance). Renewables typically only have equipment costs.

welcome.title: Welcome to Energy Simulation Platform!
welcome.description: Let's take a quick tour to help you get started with your Energy Simulation Platform.
welcome.tip: This tour will only show once. You can skip it anytime or restart it from the sidebar.

newAssessment.title: Create New Assessment
newAssessment.description: Ready to get started? Click this button to create your first Energy assessment. This will open the area selection wizard.
newAssessment.tip: When you click this, you'll enter a dedicated tour for the area selection process!

profile.title: Profile & Settings
profile.description: Access your profile, account settings, and user preferences. Manage your account and access additional options.

navigation.title: Dashboard Navigation
navigation.description: Access your main dashboard to view your projects, recent assessments, and overall statistics. This is your home base.

simulations.title: Energy Simulations
simulations.description: Create and manage your energy simulation models. Start new assessments, configure parameters, and review existing calculations.

map.title: Interactive Map
map.description: Your main workspace for geographical analysis. View map layers, select areas for assessment, and visualize energy risk data.

locations.title: Saved Locations
locations.description: Manage your saved locations and areas of interest. Quick access to frequently analyzed regions.

reports.title: Simulation Reports
reports.description: View detailed simulation reports, export data, and analyze historical trends and patterns.

technologies.title: Technologies
technologies.description: Manage energy technologies like solar panels, batteries, and heat pumps. Configure parameters and assign them to buildings.
technologies.tip: Technologies can be customized per building for accurate energy simulations.

layers.title: Map Layers
layers.description: Control map layers and overlays. Choose base maps, toggle data layers, and customize your map view.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.description: Open the platform docs for detailed guides, API references, and best practices.
documentation.tip: Opens in a new tab so you keep your current workflow.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.description: Share your thoughts and suggestions with us. Your feedback helps improve the platform.

complete.title: You're All Set!
complete.description: You've completed the tour! You're now ready to start creating Energy assessments.
complete.nextStepsDescription: Try creating your first Energy assessment or explore the interactive map.
complete.restartTourDescription: You can restart this tour anytime from the sidebar.

welcome.title: Energy Simulation Guide
welcome.description: Welcome! This tour will guide you through creating an energy simulation for your chosen region.
welcome.tip: You'll learn how to configure your model, draw areas, and create your simulation.

modelName.title: Model Name
modelName.description: Give your energy simulation model a descriptive name. This helps you identify it later in your dashboard.
modelName.tip: Good names: "Hamburg District 2024" or "Industrial Zone Energy Analysis"

dateRange.title: Simulation Period
dateRange.description: Choose the time period for your energy analysis. Select dates between 2019 and 2022 for your simulation.
dateRange.tip: Longer periods provide more comprehensive energy analysis

resolution.title: Time Resolution
resolution.description: Set the time interval for calculations. Higher resolution provides more detailed results but takes longer to process.
resolution.tip: 60 minutes is recommended for most simulations

technologies.title: Technologies & Parameters
technologies.description: Configure energy technologies (solar, batteries, heat pumps) and fine-tune advanced simulation parameters.
technologies.tip: Click to view and customize settings for your simulation

mapContainer.title: Interactive Map
mapContainer.description: This is your main workspace. Draw polygons on the map to select areas for your energy simulation.
mapContainer.tip: You can zoom and pan the map to explore different regions

search.title: Location Search
search.description: Use this search box to quickly find specific locations by name or address.
search.tip: Try typing a location name to see instant results

save.title: Create Simulation
save.description: Once you've configured everything, click here to create your energy simulation model and start the analysis.
save.tip: All fields must be completed before you can save

feedback.title: Send Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Help us improve your experience
feedback.subject: Subject
feedback.subjectPlaceholder: e.g., Map loading issue, Feature suggestion...
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Please describe your feedback, suggestions, or issues in detail...
feedback.imageDropzone: Click to upload or drag and drop
feedback.ratingDescription: How would you rate your experience?
feedback.gdprNotice: Your feedback is processed in compliance with GDPR regulations.

success.title: Feedback Submitted!
success.message: Thank you for your feedback. We'll review it and get back to you if needed.

errors.loginRequired: Please log in to submit feedback
errors.submitFailed: Failed to submit feedback. Please try again later.
errors.invalidImageType: Please select a valid image file (PNG, JPG, GIF)
errors.imageTooLarge: Image size must be less than 15MB

myFeedback.title: My Feedback
myFeedback.subtitle: Track the status of your submitted feedback
myFeedback.empty: You haven't submitted any feedback yet
myFeedback.autoDeleteNotice: This item will be automatically removed in {days} days
myFeedback.autoDeleteSoon: This item will be automatically removed soon

multiplePolygons.title: Multiple Polygons
multiplePolygons.description: Enable this option to draw multiple separate areas on the map for your Energy assessment. When disabled, drawing a new polygon will replace the previous one. When enabled, you can define multiple distinct areas that will all be included in the analysis.
multiplePolygons.example: Use this to analyze energy simulation across multiple non-contiguous areas simultaneously.

publicBuildings.title: Public Custom Buildings
publicBuildings.description: Include buildings shared by other users in the grid calculation. These are custom buildings that other users have made publicly available.
publicBuildings.example: You can also exclude individual buildings by clicking on them on the map.

privateBuildings.title: My Custom Buildings
privateBuildings.description: Include your private custom buildings in the grid calculation. These are buildings you have created and saved to your account.
privateBuildings.example: You can also exclude individual buildings by clicking on them on the map.

simulateEV.title: Simulate EV Hosting Capacity
simulateEV.description: Calculate the Electric Vehicle hosting capacity for each transformer in the selected area. This determines how many EV chargers (11 kW Level 2) can be connected before reaching grid constraints.
simulateEV.example: A 'Grid Constrained' status means the transformer is already at capacity and cannot host additional EV chargers without upgrades.

gridConstrained.title: Grid Constrained
gridConstrained.description: This status indicates that the transformer has reached its hosting capacity limit and cannot support additional EV chargers.
gridConstrained.example: Upgrade the local transformer or distribute EV charging across multiple connection points to resolve grid constraints.

scenario.title: Scenario Year
scenario.description: The target year for the energy system optimization. This determines the cost assumptions and demand projections used in the model.

co2_limit.title: CO2 Limit
co2_limit.description: The maximum allowed CO2 emissions for the optimized system in tonnes.
co2_limit.example: Lower values force the model to use more renewable energy sources.

max_hours.title: Max Battery Hours
max_hours.description: The maximum duration that battery storage can discharge at full power.
max_hours.example: 72 hours allows for multi-day storage capability.

solver.title: Optimization Solver
solver.description: The mathematical solver used to find the optimal energy system configuration.
solver.example: GLPK is a free solver, while Gurobi is a commercial high-performance solver.

autarky.title: Autarky Level
autarky.description: The degree of self-sufficiency required for the region, from 0 (no requirement) to 1 (100% self-sufficient).
autarky.example: 0.8 means 80% of energy must be produced locally.

solarPotential.title: Solar Potential Estimation
solarPotential.description: Estimated maximum PV capacity (kWp) based on total selected area.
solarPotential.example: A 1000m² area results in approx 30 kWp of potential capacity.

area.title: Total Selected Area
area.description: The combined surface area of all selected polygons on the map.

perimeter.title: Total Perimeter
perimeter.description: The total length of the boundary lines of all selected polygons.
perimeter.example: Useful for estimating fencing or cable routing requirements around the site.

estGeneration.title: Estimated Annual Generation
estGeneration.description: Predicted annual energy production based on the solar potential.
estGeneration.example: A 30 kWp system produces approx 33 MWh/year in this region.

co2Offset.title: Estimated CO₂ Offset
co2Offset.description: Potential carbon dioxide emissions avoided by using this solar energy instead of grid electricity.
co2Offset.example: Generating 100 MWh of clean energy saves approx 40 tonnes of CO₂.

voltageStatus.title: Voltage Status Indicators
voltageStatus.description: Bus voltage status is determined based on the per-unit (p.u.) voltage magnitude according to EN 50160 grid standards.
voltageStatus.example: A voltage of 0.92 p.u. shows Warning status as it is below the optimal 0.95 threshold.

pypsaSimulation.title: PyPSA Simulation
pypsaSimulation.description: Enable PyPSA (Python for Power System Analysis) to run advanced energy system optimization. When enabled, the model will optimize energy flows, storage dispatch, and technology sizing to minimize costs while meeting demand and grid constraints.
pypsaSimulation.example: Disable this for quick grid-only analysis. Enable for full energy system optimization with detailed results.

assignBuildings.title: Assign Buildings to Transformer
assignBuildings.description: Reassign buildings to a different transformer. First select the buildings you want to move, then click on the destination transformer. This is useful for load balancing or fixing overloaded transformers.
assignBuildings.example: Select overloaded buildings (red cables) and assign them to a nearby transformer with spare capacity.

buildingType.title: Building Type
buildingType.description: The building classification from OpenStreetMap (OSM). This determines the default energy profile and Standard Load Profile (SLP) class used for energy estimation.
buildingType.example: Common types: "house" (single-family), "apartments" (multi-family), "commercial", "industrial". The type affects whether residential (H0) or commercial (G0) load profiles are applied.

constructionYear.title: Construction Year
constructionYear.description: The year the building was constructed, sourced from OpenStreetMap. Used to apply age-dependent energy correction factors in AI estimation.
constructionYear.example: Buildings before 1978 use factor 1.40× (higher demand), while modern buildings (2016+) use 0.55× due to improved insulation (GEG/EnEV).

floorArea.title: External Floor Area
floorArea.description: The footprint area of the building polygon calculated from map geometry. This is the primary input for AI energy estimation — energy demand scales proportionally with floor area.
floorArea.example: A 100 m² residential building typically consumes ~3,500 kWh/year. A 200 m² building would consume approximately double.

buildingHeight.title: Building Height
buildingHeight.description: Maximum building height from OpenStreetMap geometry data. Height from ground level to the highest point of the building including roof.
buildingHeight.example: A typical 2-story residential building has a height of ~8–10 m. Data comes from available building metadata.

peakLoad.title: Peak Electrical Load
peakLoad.description: Maximum instantaneous electrical power demand. Calculated from yearly consumption divided by full load hours (FLH): Residential 1800h, Commercial 2000h, Industrial 2500h.
peakLoad.example: A building consuming 3,500 kWh/year with 1800 FLH has a peak load of ~1.94 kW.

energyDemandProfile.title: Energy Demand Profile
energyDemandProfile.description: Annual energy consumption for this building. You can manually adjust values. For multi-class buildings (mixed residential/commercial), each class is shown separately with its own editable demand.
energyDemandProfile.example: Values are in kWh/year. AI estimation provides initial values based on building type, area, and construction year.

buildingUsageType.title: Building Usage Type
buildingUsageType.description: Functional classification from OpenStreetMap tags. Determines the Standard Load Profile (SLP) class and energy intensity assumptions for demand estimation.
buildingUsageType.example: Residential types (house, apartments) use SLP H0. Commercial (retail, office) use G0-G6. Industrial uses G3.

technologies.title: Technologies
technologies.subtitle: Energy technologies for your simulations
technologies.dragDropHint: Drag and drop technologies between sections to change ownership
technologies.dropToMakeSystem: Drop here to make system tech
technologies.dropToMakeUser: Drop here to make user tech
technologies.dragInstruction: Drag a technology onto a building on the map

addModal.title: Add New Technology
addModal.description: Create a new technology with custom parameters

copyModal.title: Copy Technology
copyModal.description: Create a copy of {{name}} with a new name
copyModal.name: Name
copyModal.copyAllParameters: This will copy all {{count}} parameters from "{{name}}"

importModal.title: Import Technologies
importModal.description: Choose how to import the {{count}} technologies
importModal.existingSkipped: Existing technologies with the same key will be skipped.
importModal.systemDescription: System technologies are visible to all users and cannot be edited by non-experts.

paramModal.title: Add New Parameter
paramModal.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this parameter controls...

confirm.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteParamMessage: Are you sure you want to delete the parameter "{{name}}"?

battery_storage.name: Battery Storage
battery_storage.description: Energy storage system using batteries for load balancing and backup power

privacy.cartoDescription: To provide you with our services, we rely on CARTO and OSMF as third-party mapping providers.
privacy.sharedWithCarto: By accepting, the following details will be shared with CARTO and OSMF respectively:
privacy.localStorageDescription: Additionally, we use local storage to remember your preferences and to track whether you've seen the introductory content on this device.
privacy.reviewPolicies: For more information, please review the respective privacy policies:

regionSelector.jumpToRegion: Jump to a region with available grid data

hero.description: Model, simulate, and optimize renewable energy systems at building and district scale. EnerPlanET empowers municipalities, researchers, and energy planners to accelerate the energy transition.

features.label: Platform Features
features.title: Everything you need for energy planning
features.description: A comprehensive toolkit for modeling, simulating, and optimizing decentralized energy systems.
features.mapDesc: Visualize buildings, energy infrastructure, and grid topology in an interactive 3D environment with satellite imagery.
features.multiTechDesc: Configure PV, wind, biomass, geothermal, battery storage, and grid connections for any building or district.
features.simulationDesc: Run hourly optimization with Calliope, PyPSA, and PyLoVo solvers to find cost-optimal and carbon-minimal energy systems.
features.gridDesc: Analyze power flow, voltage profiles, transformer loading, and line capacity with integrated PyPSA and PyLoVo network simulation.
features.reportsDesc: Generate energy balance charts, load duration curves, daily profiles, cost breakdowns, and Sankey flow diagrams.
features.workspaceDesc: Share models with your team, compare simulation scenarios, and collaborate on energy transition planning.

energy.label: Energy Technologies
energy.title: Integrated renewable energy modeling
energy.description: EnerPlanET supports detailed modeling and optimization of five core energy technologies, powered by PySAM, pvlib, and Calliope.

pv.title: Solar Photovoltaic
pv.subtitle: PV Systems
pv.description: Model rooftop and ground-mounted PV systems with high-fidelity hourly simulations using PySAM and pvlib. Accounts for panel orientation, tilt, shading, inverter efficiency, and degradation.

wind.title: Wind Energy
wind.subtitle: Wind Turbines
wind.description: Simulate onshore wind turbines using PySAM wind models with MERRA-2 and Open-Meteo reanalysis data. Supports multiple turbine types, hub heights, and power curve modeling.

biomass.title: Biomass Energy
biomass.subtitle: Biomass CHP & Heating
biomass.description: Configure biomass heating and combined heat and power (CHP) systems. Supports wood chips, biogas, and organic waste feedstocks with flexible dispatch scheduling.

geothermal.title: Geothermal Energy
geothermal.subtitle: Ground Source Heat
geothermal.description: Model shallow and deep geothermal systems including ground-source heat pumps and borehole heat exchangers. Seasonal thermal storage with hourly ground temperature profiles.

battery.title: Battery Storage
battery.subtitle: Energy Storage Systems
battery.description: Optimize lithium-ion and other battery storage systems for peak shaving, self-consumption maximization, and grid services. Supports capacity sizing and degradation modeling.

howItWorks.label: Getting Started
howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.description: From region selection to actionable insights in four simple steps.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Draw or select a geographic area on the interactive map. Buildings and infrastructure are loaded automatically.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Add technologies like PV panels, wind turbines, battery storage, and biomass to buildings. Set constraints and objectives.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Launch the Calliope, PyPSA, and PyLoVo solvers to find the optimal energy system design with hourly resolution.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Explore interactive charts, energy flows, grid topology, cost breakdowns, and export comprehensive reports.

showcase.label: See it in Action
showcase.title: Powerful tools, beautiful interface
showcase.description: Designed for clarity and efficiency, EnerPlanET makes complex energy analysis accessible to everyone.
showcase.mapDesc: Explore buildings, energy systems, and grid infrastructure in a rich 3D environment with satellite imagery and real-time data overlays.
showcase.chartsDesc: Analyze energy balance, production curves, cost breakdowns, and Sankey flow diagrams with interactive charts and data export.
showcase.gridDesc: Visualize power flow, voltage profiles, transformer loading, and network topology with integrated PyPSA and PyLoVo simulation results.

trust.euDescription: EnerPlanET is a research project funded by the European Union, developed at Technische Hochschule Deggendorf in partnership with leading European universities and research institutions. The platform supports the EU's mission for climate neutrality by 2050.

contact.label: Get in Touch
contact.title: We'd love to hear from you
contact.description: Have a question, suggestion, or want to collaborate? Send us a message and our team will get back to you.
contact.name: Name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.successMessage: Thank you for reaching out. We'll get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

cta.title: Start planning your energy transition
cta.description: Join researchers and energy planners across Europe. Design sustainable energy systems with EnerPlanET.

footer.brandDescription: Advanced energy planning platform for a sustainable future. Funded by the European Union.
